name: P vs NP

framework_link.concept: Phi-irreducibility (IIT) — a system has Phi > 0 iff its joint state cannot be decomposed into independent parts without information loss

claim.statement: If P=NP under the Phi-encoding, then the certificate-verification structure of any NP-complete problem instance of size N must have Phi-irreducibility (Phi_irr) bounded by O(log N). Equivalently: a polytime verifier corresponds to a fully decomposable (Phi_irr ~ 0) information-integration graph over the certificate bits, while a genuinely NP-hard search retains super-logarithmic Phi_irr that cannot be Phi-reduced by any polytime reduction.
claim.intuition: IIT-style Phi measures the irreducibility of joint information across a partition. NP-complete instances at the SAT phase-transition (alpha = 4.26) exhibit clause-variable graphs whose information-theoretic Phi over partitions of the variable set is conjectured to grow as Theta(N) at the critical threshold. If P=NP held, there must exist a polytime witness that collapses this Phi_irr — measurable shrinkage we can probe.

falsifiability_test.name: Phi_irr_collapse_probe
falsifiability_test.design: Generate random 3-SAT instances at critical ratio alpha=4.26 for n in {6, 8, 10, 12, 14}. For each instance, compute (a) Phi_irr over the variable-partition lattice using the IIT formulation: Phi_irr(P) = MI(L_P; R_P | clauses) where (L_P, R_P) is a balanced partition of variables and MI is mutual information of satisfying-assignment marginals. (b) After running a known polytime simplifier (unit-propagation + pure-literal-elimination), recompute Phi_irr_post on the residual instance.
predictions: If P=NP claim holds in our framework: Phi_irr_post / Phi_irr_pre must shrink as n grows (collapse signature). Specifically Phi_irr_post(n) <= c*log(n) for some constant c.
If P!=NP: Phi_irr_post / Phi_irr_pre stays bounded away from 0 (no collapse), and Phi_irr_post grows super-logarithmically in n at the critical ratio.
falsifiability_test.refutation_criterion: Round-1 claim is REFUTED if measurements at n=6..14 show Phi_irr_post(n) / Phi_irr_pre(n) decreasing toward 0 with n (would force the claim to flip toward P=NP-friendly). Round-1 claim is REINFORCED (not proved) if the ratio stays >= 0.3 across all n. A NULL outcome (noisy, no monotone trend) means the Phi-encoding lacks discriminating power at this scale and round-2 must use larger n or a different partition scheme.
falsifiability_test.resource: single CPU, no GPU, no LLM

honesty.label: HEURISTIC_ATTACK_VECTOR
honesty.limitation: Phi-encoding of P vs NP is a non-standard reformulation. Even if Phi_irr collapse is observed, it does not constitute a polytime SAT algorithm — it would only suggest that the IIT framework is sensitive to NP-hard structure. Conversely, no-collapse does not prove P!=NP; it only narrows the heuristic. This is round 1 of an N-round attack.

next_round_action.round2_angle: empirical brute-force work scaling (orthogonal to Phi_irr)
next_round_action.round3_planned: scale Phi_irr probe to n=20..30 with DPLL-style polytime simplifiers (CDCL clause learning) and measure Phi_irr collapse vs solver runtime; cross-check against natural-proofs barrier (Razborov-Rudich 1997)

barriers_acknowledged: Baker-Gill-Solovay 1975 — relativization (any Phi-encoding that relativizes is doomed)
Razborov-Rudich 1997 — natural proofs (Phi_irr as a 'natural property' would be obstructed)
